articles.title: BUILDING A WEB SEARCH ENGINE FROM SCRATCH IN TWO MONTHS WITH 3 BILLION NEURAL EMBEDDINGS
articles.summary: This article goes over how to build a web search engine from scratch. It covers how to generate 3 billion SBERT embeddings using 200 GPUs, crawl and index 280 million pages, optimize query latency, and implement a service mesh. The project involved developing custom solutions for crawling, data storage with RocksDB, a sharded HNSW index for vector search, and AI-powered features like quick answers and result summarization.

articles.title: A GENTLE INTRODUCTION TO ANCHOR POSITIONING
articles.summary: Anchor positioning is a new CSS feature that allows devs to position an element (the target) relative to another element (the anchor) without relying on JavaScript. This happens by naming an anchor element with `anchor-name` and then referencing it on the target element using `position-anchor`. The target can then be positioned using either `position-area`, which places the element on a nine-square grid relative to the anchor, or the `anchor()` function, which aligns the target's edges with the anchor's edges using inset properties.

articles.title: ACCELERATING VIDEO QUALITY CONTROL AT NETFLIX WITH PIXEL ERROR DETECTION
articles.summary: Netflix is using AI to automate quality control by detecting pixel-level artifacts in videos, specifically hot pixels, reducing the need for manual reviews. It developed a neural network that analyzes video frames at full resolution to identify and locate these errors, even distinguishing them from naturally bright pixels. To train its AI model, it used a synthetic pixel error generator to create realistic training data and refined the model using real-world footage.

articles.title: LLMS AREN'T WORLD MODELS
articles.summary: LLMs do not create true "world models" despite their advanced language processing capabilities. They fail at basic tasks like tracking chess pieces or understanding color blending, which shows they don't understand the underlying concepts.

articles.title: WHY WE MIGRATED FROM NEON TO PLANETSCALE
articles.summary: OpenSecret migrated from Neon to PlanetScale for its database needs due to reliability issues and pricing concerns with Neon. The move to PlanetScale resulted in improved performance with lower latency and better API response times, along with a 38% cost reduction.

articles.title: WE KEEP REINVENTING CSS, BUT STYLING WAS NEVER THE PROBLEM
articles.summary: Despite advancements in CSS tooling, the core styling problem is from forcing a language designed for documents to fit within component-based frontend architectures, leaving devs to choose the least painful trade-off rather than finding a perfect solution.

articles.title: OMNARA
articles.summary: Omnara is a mobile-first platform that allows real-time monitoring and control of AI agents like Claude and GitHub Copilot. It provides features such as real-time monitoring, interactive Q&A, and smart notifications to improve the productivity and visibility of AI workflows. It also has remote launching and monitoring capabilities, letting users interact with their AI agents from anywhere via mobile, web, or desktop.

articles.title: CLAUDE SONNET 4 NOW SUPPORTS 1M TOKENS OF CONTEXT
articles.summary: Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 4 now supports a 1 million token context window, which gives it the ability to handle more extensive and data-intensive use cases like large-scale code analysis and document synthesis.

articles.title: BOLT
articles.summary: Bolt is a lightweight, fast, and type-safe embeddable language designed for real-time applications. It has great performance, a compact implementation, and rapid compilation speeds, making it easy to integrate into other projects.

articles.title: WIKIPEDIA LOSES CHALLENGE AGAINST ONLINE SAFETY ACT VERIFICATION RULES
articles.summary: Wikipedia has lost its legal challenge against the UK's Online Safety Act verification rules, which it feared would force it to verify user identities and compromise volunteer editor privacy. The Wikimedia Foundation argued the rules were too broad and would unfairly apply to Wikipedia, potentially requiring it to limit UK access or disable key functions. While the court rejected their arguments, the judgment emphasized Ofcom's responsibility to protect Wikipedia and left open the possibility of future legal challenges if the Act impedes Wikipedia's operations significantly.

articles.title: LINUS TORVALDS BUILT GIT IN 10 DAYS - AND NEVER IMAGINED IT WOULD LAST 20 YEARS
articles.summary: Linus Torvalds created Git in just 10 days after a conflict arose with the proprietary BitKeeper, which Linux had been using. Torvalds initially viewed Git as a potential temporary solution and didn't expect it to become the industry standard. However, Git's decentralized design and efficient synchronization made it more than just a temporary bridge.

articles.title: PERPLEXITY MAKES $34.5 BILLION OFFER FOR GOOGLE'S CHROME BROWSER
articles.summary: AI startup Perplexity made a $34.5 billion offer to acquire Chrome as a solution to a pending antitrust ruling that could force Google to sell the browser.

articles.title: A SPELLCHECKER USED TO BE A MAJOR FEAT OF SOFTWARE ENGINEERING
articles.summary: Modern programming makes idea implementation very easy, whereas in the past, even seemingly simple tasks like spellchecking needed ingenuity and resource optimization to work.

articles.title: THE BEST LINE LENGTH
articles.summary: While historical and physiological factors suggest an optimal line length range of 80-90 characters for readability and saccadic eye movement, modern practices like multi-pane editors and the structure of code itself make a slightly wider line length (like Black's 88) a reasonable default.

articles.title: DESIGN ARENA
articles.summary: Design Arena is a community-driven platform that uses blind pairwise comparisons to evaluate and rank AI design capabilities based on win rates and Elo ratings.
